courses.title: Introduction to Laboratory and Scientific Methodology

notes: The Biology major can be designed by students to include courses from a variety of life and physical sciences departments. The major is suitable for students who would like to obtain a broad knowledge of science, as well as for students preparing for careers in the health sciences. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. THIS MAJOR IS HIGHLY SELECTIVE. To be considered for admission to this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer: One year of Biology with Lab for the major, One year of General Chemistry with Lab, One year of Calculus, and One semester of Organic Chemistry with Lab. Completion of a Second semester of Organic Chemistry or one year of Calculus Based Physics (Mechanics, Electricity, and Magnetism) is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED . If your college does not offer a two semester or a three quarter sequence in calculus based physics, it is recommended that you complete Physics 5ABC at UCLA after transfer. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information about this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https:// eeb.ucla.edu/undergraduate.php and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.

UCLA's life science majors require the completion of extensive preparatory coursework prior to transfer, therefore, you must be admitted directly into a life science major; you will not be able to change from a non-life science major into a life science major after admission into UCLA. Students who have placed out of UCLA's Math 31A course based on AP/IB credit may be required to complete UCLA's Math 3C, 32A or an equivalent course after transfer. The community college statistics course included on this agreement has been approved as a substitute for Statistics 13 which means it may not transfer as an exact UCLA equivalent. Students who take Life Sciences 30A & 30B must take Life Sciences 40 or Statistics 13. UCLA's Chem 153A and 153L are upper division major courses that are recommended, but NOT required for admission into the pre-major. Transfer credit for these courses will be awarded via petition by the major department post transfer.
